content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
John F. Parkhurst 
secretary/Treasurer 
April 7, 1986 
Bob Moretti Memorial Scholarship Foundation 
925 L Street, Ste. 850 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Re: 
FPPC Advice No. A-86-091 
Dear Mr. Parkhurst: 
This is in response to your letter of March 5, 1986, which 
requested advice regarding the lobbying disclosure provisions of 
the Political Reform Act (Government Code Sections 81000 -
91015). 
Specifically, you asked whether a lobbyist may donate a 
prize to the Bo~Moretti Memorial Scholarship Foundation's 
annual golf tournament in light of the fact that a member of the 
Legislature is likely to be the recipient of the prize. 
According to your letter, the golf tournament provides the 
Foundation with its scholarship funds. 
Participants in the 
tournament are divided into 36 foursomes, each of which will 
include at least one member of the Legislature. 
Prizes are 
awarded to the 10 foursomes with t:he best golf scores. 
Government Code Section 86203 states: 
It shall be unlawful for a lobbyist, or lobbying firm, 
to make gifts to one person aggregating more than ten 
dollars ($10) in a calendar month, or to act as an 
agent or intermediary in the making of any gift, or to 
arrange for the making of any gift by any other person. 
However, under these circumstances, the prize in question is 
a gift to the Foundation and not to the person who will 
ultimately receive it. 
Therefore, a lobbyist is not prohibited 
from donating a prize for the tournament. 
However, if the 
circumstances become such that all or nearly all of the 
legislators will receive prizes, our conclusion may change. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660 


I hope the foregoing is helpful. 
Please feel free to 
contact me at (916) 322-5662 if you have additional questions. 
Sincerely, 
{!~ 
~U{v0l.L~ 
Carla Wardlow 
Political Reform Consultant 
CW:cah 


Bob Moretti Nlemorial Scholarship Foundation 
BOARD OF DIRECTORS 
Willie L. Brown, Jr. 
Chairman 
Jack Fenton 
Vice Chairman 
John Parkhurst 
Secretary/T reasu rer 
Richard Alatorre 
Bill Campbell 
Gary Hart 
Julian Lobosky 
Ken Maddy 
Marilyn Moretti 
Lou Papan 
David Roberti 
Mike Roos 
Newton Russell 
Frank Vicencia 
March 5, 1986 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
Legal Division 
Post Office Box 807 
Sacramento, California 95804 0807 
LEGAL STAFF ON PUBLIC ADVICE: 
The Bob Moretti Memorial Scholarship Foundation was established in 
1984 to honor the memory of the late Bob Moretti, former Speaker of 
the California State Assembly. 
The Foundation's purpose is 
committed to the support of scholarships in the name of Bob Moretti 
at the University of Notre Dame. 
The funds for the scholarships are obtained by a single fund raising 
golf tournament each year. 
Fees to enter the tournament are paid by 
various people including lobbyists and lobbyist employers. 
The fees 
cover the cost of golf and dinner as well as provide the scholarship 
funds. 
Members of the California Legislature are especially supportive of 
this effort. 
In fact, each of the 36 foursomes playing golf 
includes at least one member of the Legislature. 
The 10 foursomes 
with the best golf score are awarded prizes that range in value from 
a high of $200 to a low of $10. 
These prizes are donated to the 
Foundation for use with the tournament by many companies and 
individuals. 
We would appreciate your addressing the following 
question: 
Can a lobbyist make a personal contribution to the 
Foundation in the form of a prize that will, in all probability, be 
won by a group that will include a member of the California 
Legislature without violating the provisions of the Political Reform 
Act of 1974. 
Thank you for your consideration. 
Please call Gregg Cook 325~7943 
if you have any questions or desire further clarification of this 
request. 
925 L STREET, SUITE 850 • 
SACRAMENTO, CA 95814 
~2"
